game_name: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate 2D sidescrolling levels with dual-character switching mechanic, defeat enemies via jumping/cartwheeling/throwing, reach level exit while collecting hidden items and bonuses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Maintaining control of both characters through enemy encounters; managing character positioning and ability selection (Diddy's speed vs Dixie's jump height and hair glide); discovering and completing optional bonus areas
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescue Donkey Kong from King K. Rool by mastering creative platforming challenges across diverse island environments, leveraging animal companion abilities and environmental mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Forced character switching on hit (losing active character, swapping to partner) creates high-consequence positioning gameplay; complex bonus area unlock design (hidden walls, character-specific barrels, cannonball activation) rewards exploration and mastery; emphasis on optional 102% completion (all levels, bosses, bonus challenges, DK coins, Kong family visits) suggests designer commitment to content density and replayability value

difficulty.lives_system: Standard life counter; player loses a life when both characters die; extra lives earned via balloons, 100 banana collection, or KONG letter collection
difficulty.checkpointing: Star-painted barrel checkpoints within levels; players restart from checkpoint or level beginning on character death; no mid-level checkpoint saves detected in speedrun data
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No documented difficulty settings; challenge is inherent to level design. 102% completion goal provides optional hard-mode objective. Speedrun leaderboards (37m any%, 1h27m 102%) indicate high skill ceiling with tight execution windows

progression.upgrade_system: Animal companions with unique abilities (Rambi: charge attacks; Squawks: flight/egg spit; Rattly: extreme jump height; Squitter: web traversal; Enguarde: swordfish combat/wall breaking). Acquired within levels via special barrels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Kremcoin collection gates Lost World access (15 Kremcoins required to proceed with Klubba). Level-by-level world progression required. Kong family members (Cranky, Wrinkly, Swanky, Funky) unlock in sequence providing hints, education, quiz rewards, and world-switching flights
replayability_hooks: 102% completion target (all levels, bosses, bonus challenges, DK coins, Kong family visits)
Hidden bonus areas behind breakable walls and character-specific barrel activation
DK coin collection for Lost World and True Ending access
Multiple speedrun categories (any%, warpless, 102%, glitchless, no team-up, blindfolded)
Animal companion ability mastery unlocks level shortcuts

world_building_techniques: Environmental design: eight distinct world themes (jungle, circus, water, mine, factory, etc.) with thematic enemy placement and obstacle types
NPC dialogue: Cranky Kong provides hints and lore; Wrinkly Kong offers educational guidance; Swanky Kong runs quiz show
Character introduction: Dixie Kong's first franchise appearance; Wrinkly Kong debut as elder Kong family member
Level naming: themed level titles reinforce world identity (e.g., Web Woods, Barrel Bayou, Toxic Tower)
Kidnapping setup: King K. Rool captures Donkey Kong, establishing rescue narrative framing

boss_design.philosophy: One mandatory boss per world culminating progression; boss encounters serve as gatekeepers requiring mastery of world-specific mechanics and character abilities
notable_bosses: Kaptain K. Rool (final boss, K. Rool Duel and Krocodile Kore encounters)
World bosses (8 total, one per world, names not documented in provided sources)

faction_indicators: Kremling Krew: reptilian antagonists (Kaptain K. Rool leadership); enemy variety suggests organized military structure
Kong Family: Diddy, Dixie, Donkey, Cranky, Wrinkly, Swanky, Funky as allied faction
Klubba's neutral merchant faction: demands Kremcoins for Lost World passage (independent operator)
danger_signals: King K. Rool's kidnapping of Donkey Kong signals active threat escalation
Crocodile Isle setting controlled by Kremling forces
Environmental hazards (lava, toxic water, beehives) indicate hostile terrain or creature territories
Boss placement at world ends marks territorial strongholds requiring defeat to advance
environmental_storytelling: Crocodile Isle geography: eight distinct biomes representing conquered or contested territories
Castle levels (Castle Crush, Chain Link Chamber, Toxic Tower, Stronghold Showdown) indicate Kremling fortification infrastructure
Animal companion presence suggests alliance networks and domestication
Bonus area design (hidden walls, secret passages) implies resistance infrastructure or lost treasure caches
